evals.prompt: Help me write a commit message for my current changes
evals.expected_output: A conventional commit message based on the actual diff in the repository
expectations: The output contains a commit message in conventional commits format: type(scope): description
The type is one of: feat, fix, docs, style, refactor, perf, test, build, ci, chore, revert
The description is in lowercase imperative mood
The subject line is under 72 characters

evals.prompt: What should I commit? Summarize my recent changes
evals.expected_output: A summary of changes with a proposed conventional commit message
expectations: The skill analyzes the git diff or status
A conventional commit message is proposed
If changes span multiple concerns, the skill suggests splitting commits

evals.prompt: Prepare a conventional commit for the bug fix I just made
expectations: The commit message starts with fix or fix(scope):
The description accurately reflects the changes in the diff
The message is ready to copy and use directly
